user_query:
I manage a small community services portal and I’m tired of hand-wavy CI notes that don’t line up with what actually ran. In this local repo, I need you to make the basic CI pipeline unambiguous, run it, and then draft a plain‑text deployment summary that cites concrete outputs. Requirements: 1) Modify the pipeline config at ci/ci.json to add a second step named "tests" that runs the exact command: python project/tests.py. Keep the existing "build" step as the first step and do not remove or rename it. 2) Run the pipeline via the provided runner: python tools/ci_runner.py. This must produce (at minimum) the following artifacts if everything is wired correctly: - out/build/build_info.json - out/build/app.txt - out/test_results.json - out/logs/ci.log (aggregated run log from the runner) 3) Draft a concise, plain‑text deployment summary message and save it to out/deploy_summary_email.txt with the following minimal structure for reliable verification: - First line: Subject: Community Portal CI summary - Body must include these single‑line fields anywhere in the message (exact key labels): - Commit: - Version: - Built-At: - Tests: total=, passed=

, failed= The Commit value must match both the content of project/COMMIT and the commit field in out/build/build_info.json. The Version and Built-At values must match out/build/build_info.json. The test counts must match out/test_results.json. Add one short, clear paragraph for non‑technical staff explaining what was produced and whether it’s safe to proceed, but do not pad it with vague reassurances. Input paths you must use: - Pipeline config: ci/ci.json - Runner: tools/ci_runner.py - Build script and metadata: project/build.py, project/VERSION, project/COMMIT - App and tests: project/app.py, project/tests.py Expected outputs and where to write them: - Run log: out/logs/ci.log - Build artifact metadata: out/build/build_info.json and out/build/app.txt - Test results: out/test_results.json - Drafted summary message: out/deploy_summary_email.txt Please do not introduce external dependencies or assume any remote services—this should run locally as provided. No fuzzy summaries: ensure all reported values in the email are directly consistent with the generated files. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
